questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the primary objective and administrative body of the Kenton R. Miller Award?

options.text: It is a biennial award presented by the IUCN to individuals demonstrating innovation in conserving national parks and protected areas.
It is an annual grant provided by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) to developing nations for biodiversity conservation.
It is an award conferred by the World Wildlife Fund (WWF) to indigenous communities protecting forest reserves.
It is a recognition by UNESCO for the best-managed biosphere reserves globally.

questions.explanation: The Kenton R. Miller Award is presented every two years by the IUCN's World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA). It honors individuals who show innovation and leadership in conserving national parks and protected areas.
questions.takeaway: Recognizing the administrative body (IUCN-WCPA) and the target recipient (individuals, not institutions or countries) is crucial for distinguishing this award from other global environmental honors.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Constituted by: World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA), one of six technical commissions of IUCN. Established in 2006 for Innovation in National Parks and Protected Area Sustainability. Honors individuals who show innovation and leadership in conserving national parks and protected areas. Presented every two years by the IUCN-WCPA.
